Automotive electronics manufacturing service

From the sensor on the bumper to the power module in the drivetrain, Hana builds the electronics that go into vehicles — assembled to automotive quality standards, across four countries, under one group.

Program continuity

The Hana automotive advantage

Automotive buyers measure a supplier on quality discipline, traceability and resilience. Here is where Hana stands.

Automotive quality system

IATF 16949 lines with PPAP and APQP discipline — the documentation and process control automotive programs require, not bolted on after the fact.

Traceable by default

Material, unit and product traceability captured at every step, so a field question can be traced back to a board, a reel and a shift.

Resilient footprint

The same processes are qualified across China, Thailand, Cambodia and the USA — so a program can shift sites for capacity or supply continuity.

Relationships that last

Some automotive programs have run with Hana for over twenty years. Continuity of people and process is part of the quality story.

Automotive products we produce

Five areas cover what we build for vehicles today — from the power that moves them to the boards and tags inside. Each is qualified on automotive lines.

Power Modules

Die attach, sintering and clip/wire bonding for IGBT and SiC power modules — built for the current and heat of electrified powertrains.

Automotive — frequently asked

Common questions from automotive engineering and procurement teams.

Automotive lines run to IATF 16949, with PPAP and APQP discipline. Parts are qualified to the relevant AEC-Q stress qualifications for the device type.

Have an automotive program in mind?

Tell us what the part does in the vehicle and your target volumes. We’ll point you to the right line and come back with a plan.
